key_insight: CALM learns interpretable links between brain regions and genetic pathways from separate unimodal datasets by aligning them in a shared latent space.
clinical_relevance: This could help researchers uncover biology relevant to neuropsychiatric disease using widely available single-modality datasets, even when matched imaging-genetics samples are scarce.
method: Uses linear projections to map imaging ROIs and genetic pathways into a shared latent space.
Matches class-conditional latent distributions while keeping groups separable.
Produces interpretable pathway–ROI associations that can be checked against prior literature.
evidence_signal: In experiments on autism spectrum disorder, CALM outperformed several state-of-the-art methods and ablations, and its learned associations remained stable versus a paired baseline.
watch_item: Findings depend on dataset alignment and model assumptions, so associations should be validated in independent paired cohorts before broader use.
paper_title: CALM: Interpretable Cross-Modal Alignment for Biomarker Discovery from Unpaired Data
